title: Open Problem of Illingworth–Scott–Wood on absolute constant treewidth bound
statement_text: Is $f(\mathcal{G})$ upper bounded by an absolute constant, where $f(\mathcal{G})$ is the minimum integer such that for some $c$, every $n$-vertex graph $G\in\mathcal{G}$ is contained in $H\boxtimes K_{\!m}$ for some graph $H$ with treewidth at most $f(\mathcal{G})$, with $m\leqslant c\sqrt{n}$?
context_text: Illingworth, Scott and Wood introduced the parameter $f(\mathcal{G})$ for a proper minor-closed class $\mathcal{G}$ and showed $f(\mathcal{G}_t)\leqslant t-2$ for the class $\mathcal{G}_t$ of $K_t$-minor-free graphs. They asked whether this parameter is bounded by a constant independent of $t$.
paper_contribution: Proves affirmatively that $f(\mathcal{G})\leqslant 4$ for every proper minor-closed class $\mathcal{G}$, by showing every $n$-vertex $K_t$-minor-free graph is contained in $H\boxtimes K_{\!m}$ for some graph $H$ of treewidth at most $4$, where $m\in O_t(\sqrt{n})$.
paper_title: Product structure extension of the Alon--Seymour--Thomas theorem

title: Open Problem of Illingworth–Scott–Wood on planar graph product structure
statement_text: Is $f(\mathcal{L})\leqslant 2$, where $\mathcal{L}$ is the class of planar graphs?
context_text: Illingworth, Scott and Wood showed $f(\mathcal{L})\leqslant 3$ as a consequence of planar graphs being $K_5$-minor-free, and posed the open problem of whether the treewidth could be reduced to $2$ in the product structure decomposition of planar graphs.
paper_contribution: Resolves the problem by proving that every $n$-vertex planar graph is contained in $H\boxtimes K_{\!m}$ where $H$ has treewidth $2$ and $m\in O(\sqrt{n})$; the treewidth bound of $2$ is best possible.
paper_title: Product structure extension of the Alon--Seymour--Thomas theorem
